mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings and lifts S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The 12-month rolling Rank IC of RSAFS monthly change with 20-day forward returns is below 0.02, or the top-quintile RSAFS growth portfolios underperform bottom-quintile over a 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate drops below 1%, it signals that the Federal Reserve has entered an aggressive easing cycle, compressing discount rates and lifting equity prices over the next month.
failure_condition: In instances where FEDFUNDS is below 1.0, the average 20-day forward return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10), or more than 50% of such instances yield negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate and decline in housing starts indicates a contraction in household wealth and demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: When both UNRATE is rising and HOUST is falling (concurrent quarter-on-quarter decline in HOUST and increase in UNRATE), the forward 60-day return is positive in more than 40% of cases over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a low interest rate environment (FEDFUNDS below 2%), rising consumer price inflation signals overheating that will eventually provoke monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter; the opposite holds in high-rate environments where rising inflation indicates strong demand.
failure_condition: In low-rate regimes, the 60-day forward return following a 3-month CPI acceleration > 0.5% is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10) or the signal reverses sign in different rate regimes as measured by rolling subperiod analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Real industrial production growth reflects expansion in the manufacturing sector, which leads to higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: The weekly return prediction based on INDPRO month-over-month change has a negative hit ratio over any 1-year rolling window, or the information coefficient is not statistically different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5%, it signals a weakening labor market that depresses consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: In all months where UNRATE is above 5.5%, the average 20-day return is not statistically negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the hit rate of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising inflation (CPI increase) and slowing economic growth (GDP deceleration) creates a stagflationary environment that severely depresses equity valuations, predicting negative returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month change > 1% and GDPC1 quarterly growth < 0.5%
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met, the 60-day forward return is positive in more than 30% of occurrences, or the unconditional return in such periods is not significantly lower than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle (when FEDFUNDS is sequentially increasing), strong payroll employment growth confirms economic resilience and supports positive S&P 500 returns over a short horizon; in a loosening cycle, it may signal a late-cycle overvaluation and predict negative returns. This hypothesis applies in the tightening regime.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS increasing over past 6 months)
failure_condition: In months classified as tightening cycles, the 5-day forward return following a payroll increase above 100,000 is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or the signal's direction is not consistent across tightening cycles.
